prompt:
FIXED FOREGROUND SUBJECT (locked layer): the attached image is a locked foreground layer. Reproduce the woman and her dress EXACTLY and identically — do NOT redraw, recolor, restyle, resize, reshape, or move them. Keep face, skin tone, hair (dark, centre-parted, low sleek bun), the single gold hoop earring, soft natural makeup, expression, hands and full pose pixel-identical. Keep the dress exactly: a cream/ivory cotton midi dress with an all-over small ditsy floral print in muted rust-red, dusty pink, ochre and sage-green on a cream ground, WHITE crochet/lace trim on the V-neckline and flutter cap sleeves, smocked waist — preserve print, lace trim and drape precisely.

Generate ONLY a new photographic background, fully replacing the existing beige plaster wall and cream curtain.
CRITICAL — PHYSICAL CONTACT: she is leaning her left shoulder and upper back against a surface. Build a solid, substantial structure (described below) EXACTLY at that contact line so she is genuinely resting against it with her weight settling in. Render a soft, realistic contact shadow where her shoulder, arm and hip meet the surface. She must read as truly leaning on something solid — NOT floating, NOT pasted or 'banged' into the scene.
CRITICAL — SEPARATION: the surface directly behind her torso must be a DEEPER or CONTRASTING tone than her cream floral dress so she stands out clearly. Do NOT place a pale cream wall directly behind her body.
AUTHENTIC: real weathered materials, natural daylight, believable imperfections; photoreal, documentary-editorial; avoid any over-rendered, glossy or artificial look. Shallow depth of field, background softly out of focus, no text.

STRUCTURE/BACKGROUND: she leans her left shoulder and back against an aged OCHRE-TERRACOTTA plaster wall, right beside a weathered dusty-blue wooden shutter. The deeper warm wall and the blue shutter separate the cream dress. A potted olive at the base. Provencal courtyard in true afternoon daylight.
